answers.answer: A twelve-week pop-up is reversible, addresses the travel barrier, and avoids spending the reserve on a lease before demand and transfer reliability are demonstrated.
Advance reservations reduce spontaneity, but they keep satellite stock small and let staff check exactly which items should move between sites.
Delivery was rejected because insurance, address handling, and unpredictable route time add recurring obligations that the room-based pilot avoids.
I used the same volunteer rotation, but I cannot reconstruct evidence that six volunteers can cover three new afternoons without reducing the current storefront schedule.
